title: some/none: evaluator
analysis:
Task 163 adds evaluation support for `some(expr)` and `none` expression forms in the reify-expr evaluator. Task 162 (dependency, status: done) adds `CompiledExprKind::OptionSome(Box<CompiledExpr>)` and `CompiledExprKind::OptionNone` variants to the enum in reify-types, along with constructor helpers, walk/collect_value_refs/remap_entity arms. This task adds the corresponding match arms in `eval_expr` and comprehensive tests.

The core semantics:
- `none` evaluates to `Value::Option(None)`
- `some(expr)` evaluates the inner expr and wraps: `Value::Option(Some(Box::new(eval(expr))))`
- Critically, `some(undef)` produces `Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Undef)))` — it does NOT propagate Undef. This preserves the three-way distinction: `Value::Undef` (error/missing), `Value::Option(None)` (intentional absence), `Value::Option(Some(Value::Undef))` (present but value undefined).

The implementation is small (2-4 lines per variant in eval_expr) but the test suite must verify the subtle Undef-wrapping semantics thoroughly. Tests follow the established pattern in crates/reify-expr/tests/ (lambda_eval_tests.rs, quantifier_eval_tests.rs, etc.).

Existing infrastructure: `Value::Option(Option<Box<Value>>)` already exists with PartialEq, Ord, Display, content_hash. Test helpers `reify_test_support::values::{some_val, none_val, undef}` exist. `CompiledExpr::literal()` and `CompiledExpr::value_ref()` are available for building test expressions. Task 162 should provide `CompiledExpr::option_some()` and/or `CompiledExpr::option_none()` constructors; if not, tests can construct CompiledExpr structs directly.

prerequisites.description: Verify task 162 (parser/compiler) has landed: CompiledExprKind::OptionSome and OptionNone variants exist in crates/reify-types/src/expr.rs with walk/collect_value_refs/remap_entity arms and constructor helpers. If constructors are missing, they must be added before tests can be written cleanly.
prerequisites.blocker_note: Task 162 is marked done in task system but OptionSome/OptionNone variants are absent from codebase (main at 5256c4c and all branches). Escalated as esc-163-25.

steps.description: Create crates/reify-expr/tests/option_eval_tests.rs with 2 failing tests: (a) eval_none_produces_option_none — construct a CompiledExprKind::OptionNone expression, evaluate with eval_expr, assert result == Value::Option(None). (b) eval_none_is_not_undef — assert the result of evaluating none is not Value::Undef (verifying Option(None) != Undef at the evaluator level).
Add OptionNone match arm in eval_expr in crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs: `CompiledExprKind::OptionNone => Value::Option(None)`. Tests from step-1 pass.

Add 3 failing tests: (a) eval_some_wraps_int — OptionSome(literal(Int(5))) evaluates to Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Int(5)))). (b) eval_some_wraps_bool — OptionSome(literal(Bool(true))) evaluates to Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Bool(true)))). (c) eval_some_wraps_real — OptionSome(literal(Real(3.14))) evaluates to Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Real(3.14)))).

Add OptionSome match arm in eval_expr: `CompiledExprKind::OptionSome(inner) => { let val = eval_expr(inner, ctx); Value::Option(Some(Box::new(val))) }`. IMPORTANT: No Undef short-circuit — wrapping Undef is intentional semantics. Tests from step-3 pass.

Add 3 verification tests for Undef semantics (should pass with step-4 impl): (a) eval_some_undef_produces_option_some_undef — some(literal(Undef)) yields Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Undef))), NOT Value::Undef. (b) eval_some_undef_not_equal_to_none — assert some(undef) != Value::Option(None). (c) eval_some_undef_not_equal_to_bare_undef — assert some(undef) != Value::Undef.

Add 4 verification tests for nesting and value refs (should pass with step-4 impl): (a) eval_some_nested_some — some(some(Int(3))) yields Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Int(3))))))). (b) eval_some_wraps_none — some(none) yields Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Option(None)))). (c) eval_some_value_ref — some(value_ref(x)) where x=Int(42) in ValueMap yields Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Int(42)))). (d) eval_some_missing_ref_wraps_undef — some(value_ref(x)) where x is absent from ValueMap yields Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Undef))), not bare Undef.

design_decisions.decision: No Undef propagation in OptionSome evaluation
design_decisions.rationale: The task description explicitly requires some(undef) to produce Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Undef))), which is distinct from both Value::Option(None) and Value::Undef. This preserves a three-way distinction: error state (Undef), intentional absence (None), and present-but-undefined (Some(Undef)). Unlike BinOp/FunctionCall which short-circuit on Undef, OptionSome must NOT propagate Undef — it wraps whatever the inner expression evaluates to.

Following the established pattern: lambda_eval_tests.rs, quantifier_eval_tests.rs, collection_eval_tests.rs each cover a distinct feature area. Option evaluation gets its own file for consistency and clear module boundary.

design_decisions.decision: Rely on task 162 for CompiledExprKind variants and constructor helpers
design_decisions.rationale: Task 162 (parser/compiler) adds OptionSome/OptionNone variants to CompiledExprKind. Rust's exhaustive match means walk/collect_value_refs/remap_entity arms must be added at the same time. Constructors are likely added for the compiler's use. If constructors are absent, tests can build CompiledExpr structs directly using the kind enum and ContentHash.

reuse.how: Build inner expressions for some(literal_value) test cases
Build inner expressions for some(value_ref) test cases with ValueMap
Construct expected Value::Option values in test assertions — saves verbose Box::new wrapping
Standard way to create eval context for tests with no user-defined functions
Already exists with PartialEq, Ord, Display, content_hash — no changes needed to Value enum
Same imports (reify_expr::{eval_expr, EvalContext}, reify_types::{CompiledExpr, Type, Value, ValueCellId, ValueMap}), helper structure, and assertion patterns
